name: 3-Step Pop Jump
source: Chris Atkinson · Y3–Y4 long jump session card

ks1.objective: Combine knee drive and quick take-off in a low-stakes context, no pit.

lks2.objective: Combine knee drive, arm action, and quick take-off — the last rehearsal before the pit.

uks2.objective: Polish full take-off coordination — speed maintained through the take-off, full extension.
successCriteria: Full hip and knee extension at take-off

equipment.item: Open grass or runway behind for run-up

space.description: 5–7 m clear lane. Cone marks the take-off line. Children run AT the cone, take off, land safely on both feet, walk back.

steps: Mark a take-off line with one cone. Stand each child 3 strides back from it.
Coach demos: 3 running steps — "one, two, three..." — then on the 4th step, take off from ONE foot. Land on both feet.
Cue the 3 phases of the take-off: (1) DRIVE the free knee up high, (2) PUNCH the opposite arm to the sky, (3) QUICK TAKE-OFF — push the ground hard and fast.
First child runs the 3 strides, takes off, lands, walks back.
Each child gets 2 attempts. Queue at the back between turns.
Coach watches for ONE thing per round and announces it (eg. "this round I'm watching for the KNEE DRIVE").

cues.trigger: as the rhythm cue — coach calls the count
if they brake before the line

errors.cause: Hesitation — doesn't trust the strong leg
errors.fix: "ONE foot only — your STRONG foot." Ask them which foot feels right, then let them choose.

errors.cause: Braking instinct — wants to feel safe
errors.fix: "Same speed all the way — don't BRAKE!" Coach jogs alongside for a demo.
"PUNCH the sky with the opposite arm!" Pause and rehearse the arm action standing still first.

errors.cause: Not pre-loaded; hasn't internalised the knee drive
errors.fix: "Drive the knee UP — high and FAST." Refer back to Giant Walks.

errors.cause: Too much height, not enough forward; or sits back on landing
errors.fix: "Forward, not just up — feet to the front when you land."

progressions: Walk-in version: 3 walking steps, no run — gets the foot pattern.
Add chalk lines for foot placement — visual targets.
Pop OVER a low cone (10–15 cm) for a knee-drive visual target.
Strong: 5-step approach into the same pop.
questions: Why do we count "one-two-three" before we jump?
What's your ARM doing as you take off?
base.showmanship: This is the LAST rehearsal before the pit. Build excitement — "this is the SAME jump you'll do in the pit, just no sand!" Pump the energy up. The transition into the main activity should feel inevitable.
